Name: Field Distinguishability
ShortDescription: Minimal admissible pattern of sustained difference arising within the proto-field of admissibility, enabling comparison and subsequent differentiation.
Description: Field Distinguishability is the first admissible structural pattern of repeatable difference arising within the Proto-field of Admissibility. It introduces the minimal condition under which one region, state, or excitation can be structurally distinguished from another without presupposing metric space, temporal flow, or fully developed physical operators. The arising does not denote a material object, localized carrier, or completed effect. It denotes a reproducible ontological pattern of difference-preservation that makes comparison possible and thereby opens the admissible basis for later temporal distinction, spatial distinction, compensation relations, and localization structures. Within SOMA this arising functions as a methodological foundational pattern rather than an empirically isolated physical entity.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational ontological reconstruction within SOMA; prerequisite for admissible comparison and later operator formation.

Name: Temporal Distinction
ShortDescription: System-level property of directed sequential differentiation, enabling ordered variation within the admissible field.
Description: Temporal Distinction denotes the admissible property by which distinguishable configurations can be ordered along a directed sequence. It does not presuppose physical time, measurable duration, or a metric continuum. Instead, it expresses the minimal ordered differentiability required for successive states to be structurally compared as earlier and later within the pre-metric Proto-field of Admissibility as already rendered distinguishable by Field Distinguishability. Within SOMA this arising is treated as a property rather than a process, because it characterizes the field as possessing an axis of ordered variation rather than constituting a completed transformation in itself. Temporal Distinction provides the ontological basis for directed change, later dynamical description, and the admissibility of compensatory and action-like structures at Level 0.

Evidence.Reference: Structural reconstruction of admissible ordered variation in pre-metric ontological frameworks; prerequisite for later dynamical formalization.

Name: Spatial Distinction
ShortDescription: System-level property of directional contrast across admissible variation, enabling localization within the field.
Description: Spatial Distinction denotes the admissible property by which distinguishable configurations can be differentiated across an abstract axis of separation. It does not presuppose physical space, metric extension, or geometric coordinates. Instead, it expresses the minimal structural condition under which contrast can be localized and compared as separation within the pre-metric Proto-field of Admissibility as already rendered distinguishable by Field Distinguishability. Within SOMA this arising is treated as a property rather than a process, because it characterizes the field as possessing an axis of localization rather than constituting a completed transformation in itself. Spatial Distinction provides the ontological basis for gradients, positional differentiation, and later configurational stabilization at Level 0.

Evidence.Reference: Structural reconstruction of admissible localization in pre-metric ontological frameworks; prerequisite for later gradient and configurational formalization.

Name: Compensation Energy
ShortDescription: System-level scalar property expressing the compensatory cost required to stabilize admissible distortions within the field.
Description: Compensation Energy denotes the admissible scalar property by which the already differentiated admissibility field registers the structural cost of sustaining and stabilizing directed distortions. It does not yet denote physical energy as a conserved substance, measurable thermodynamic quantity, or fully dynamical variable. Instead, it functions as a compensatory property that aggregates admissible deviation into a unified scalar measure of structural tension. Given temporal distinction and spatial distinction, this arising binds ordered variation and localized contrast into a common compensatory frame, making it possible to evaluate stabilization demand, compare admissible configurations, and prepare later variational selection. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a property because it characterizes the field through a derived scalar attribute rather than constituting an object, process, or completed effect.

Evidence.Reference: Structural reconstruction of compensatory scalar measures in pre-metric admissibility frameworks; prerequisite for later variational and stability formalization.

Name: Admissible Wave Coherence
ShortDescription: Dynamically persistent coherence regime in which oscillatory variation remains structurally stable under Level-0 admissibility constraints.
Description: Admissible Wave Coherence denotes the admissible process by which oscillatory variation remains structurally persistent under ordered temporal distinction, localized spatial distinction, and compensatory scalar tension. Its stability is not only continuous but also becomes compatible with discrete compensatory selection, allowing repeatable bounded modes rather than arbitrarily diffuse oscillatory support. It is not yet a physical wave in the empirical field-theoretic sense and does not presuppose experimentally established wave equations. Instead, it marks the first dynamically sustained coherence regime within the Level-0 admissibility framework, where recognizable oscillatory form can persist through variation without collapsing into undifferentiated fluctuation. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a process because its identity lies in ongoing coherent variation rather than in a bounded object or a static property. This arising provides the formal dynamical carrier required for later resonance closure, localization criteria, and node-like persistence.

Evidence.Reference: Variational admissibility and structural reconstruction of coherent oscillatory modes as prerequisites for resonance and localization at Level 0.

Name: Admissibility Variational Functional
ShortDescription: Structural pattern of a variational admissibility functional that filters permissible Level-0 configurations.
Description: Admissibility Variational Functional denotes the structural pattern by which temporal distinction, spatial distinction, compensation energy, and admissible wave coherence are integrated into a single variational admissibility form. This functional operates over configurations already constrained by the Minimal Geometric Admissibility Frame, so that admissibility selection is not purely algebraic but geometrically bounded and coherence under conditions where compensatory balance can be selected in repeatable discrete portions. It is not a physical Lagrangian in the established field-theoretic sense and does not claim empirical law status. Instead, it acts as a formal filtering rule that distinguishes admissible from inadmissible Level-0 configurations by organizing how variation, gradient structure, and compensatory cost can coherently combine. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a pattern because it expresses a reproducible structural rule of filtering rather than a specific object, ongoing process, or derived property. This arising provides the formal backbone for later resonance conditions, localization mechanisms, and configurational closure at Level 0.

Evidence.Reference: Variational admissibility frameworks and invariant selection principles for coherent Level-0 configurations.

Name: Triadic Resonant Closure (CRT Condition)
ShortDescription: Structural pattern of triadic resonant closure forming the first nontrivial coherent contour within Level-0 admissibility.
Description: Triadic Resonant Closure (CRT Condition) denotes the structural pattern by which three admissibly coherent oscillatory modes enter a closed resonant relation. This arising is not a directly asserted physical interaction law and not a single empirical event. It is the formal triadic closure condition under which three modes mutually reinforce and constrain one another such that the contour cannot be reduced to independent pairwise relations. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a pattern because it expresses a reproducible structural condition of closure rather than a localized object or a scalar property. The CRT condition establishes the first nontrivial coherent contour at Level 0 and provides the formal basis for later configurational density, resonant localization, and node formation.

Evidence.Reference: Nonlinear triadic resonance closure and interaction motifs in admissibility-based field reconstruction; prerequisite for localization and node formation at Level 0.

Name: Configurational Density
ShortDescription: Invariant scalar property measuring the localization and coherence of a closed resonant configuration.
Description: Configurational Density measures the concentration of a closure regime that has become sufficiently integrable to sustain localized admissibility rather than merely transient coherent overlap. It is not a physical mass density, probability density, thermodynamic density, or material concentration. This concentration is grounded in a closure regime whose compensatory support is discretely stabilizable rather than indefinitely continuous. Instead, it functions as an invariant configurational measure that aggregates the resonance structure into a single descriptor of how concentrated and coherent the contour has become under admissible transformation. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a property because it characterizes the state of a resonant configuration rather than constituting a new object or an independent dynamic process. Configurational Density provides the first proto-localization criterion at Level 0 and prepares the transition from closed resonant contour to node-like persistence.

Evidence.Reference: Invariant density measures for localization in resonant field configurations; structural criterion for proto-localization at Level 0.

Name: Resonant Localized Configuration (CRT-Node)
ShortDescription: Persistent localized configuration formed by closed triadic resonance and stabilized by internal feedback and configurational concentration.
Description: Resonant Localized Configuration (CRT-Node) denotes the admissible object-like formation obtained when a closed triadic resonant contour achieves sufficient configurational concentration and internal feedback stability to persist as a localized unit. It is the first object-like unit at Level 0 with bounded identity, local support, and internal coherence. The CRT-node is not a classical particle and not an empirically asserted microscopic constituent of nature. It is a persistent resonant configuration whose stability depends on the closed coupling of constituent modes and the maintained concentration of the underlying configuration. Within SOMA this arising is classified as an object because it can be referenced as a stable localized unit that remains identifiable under small perturbations and can participate as a carrier in subsequent nonlinear interaction, ensemble formation, and higher-order closure.

Evidence.Reference: Closed-loop resonance and localization criteria in admissibility-based field reconstruction; structural basis for the first persistent localized unit at Level 0.

Name: Nonlinear CRT Variational Functional
ShortDescription: Structural pattern of a nonlinear variational functional that encodes self-sustaining resonant localization in the CRT-node.
Description: Nonlinear CRT Variational Functional denotes the structural pattern by which the general admissibility variational functional is extended to capture the nonlinear closure specific to a resonant localized configuration. It is not a physical interaction law and not an empirically fixed field equation. Instead, it is a formal variational pattern expressing how constituent resonant modes of the CRT-node are coupled so that localization, bounded amplitude, and internal reinforcement remain stable under perturbation. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a pattern because it defines a reproducible structural rule for nonlinear resonant stabilization rather than constituting an object, a scalar property, or a time-extended process in itself. This arising marks the transition from passive resonant closure to actively stabilized node coherence and prepares the basis for node-node interaction and ensemble-scale organization at Level 0.

Evidence.Reference: Nonlinear resonance closure and cubic coupling motifs in admissibility-based variational frameworks; structural basis for robust CRT-node stabilization.

Name: CRT-Node Ensemble
ShortDescription: Coordinated object composed of multiple interacting resonant localized nodes within a shared admissibility architecture.
Description: CRT-Node Ensemble denotes the admissible object-like formation obtained when multiple resonant localized configurations become organized into a coordinated multi-node arrangement within a shared admissibility architecture. It is not a mere aggregate of independent CRT-nodes. It is a structured ensemble whose members remain distinguishable while participating in stable relations of mutual influence, compatibility, and collective organization. Within SOMA this arising is classified as an object because the ensemble can be referenced as a persistent higher-order unit with identifiable internal composition and bounded organizational coherence. The ensemble establishes the first collective object at Level 0 and provides the carrier basis for ensemble-level variational structure, synchronization modes, and higher-order configurational dynamics.

Evidence.Reference: Ensemble formation and cooperative coupling in admissibility-based resonant node dynamics; structural basis for collective organization at Level 0.

Name: Ensemble-Level Variational Functional
ShortDescription: Structural pattern of a higher-order variational functional governing cross-node interactions within a coordinated CRT-node ensemble.
Description: Ensemble-Level Variational Functional denotes the structural pattern by which interactions among multiple resonant localized configurations are integrated into a single higher-order admissibility form. It is not a physical law and not a standalone dynamical process. Instead, it expresses the ensemble-level rule by which cross-node coupling, compatibility constraints, compensatory redistribution, and collective phase organization are structurally coordinated. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a pattern because it defines a reproducible higher-order organizational form rather than a bounded object or derived scalar property. This arising establishes the variational architecture required for coherent ensemble-level regulation, synchronization modes, and the later emergence of ensemble-scale quanta at Level 0.

Evidence.Reference: Ensemble-level variational frameworks and collective admissibility in multi-node resonant systems; structural basis for synchronization and ensemble-scale coherence at Level 0.

Name: Ensemble Synchronization Mode
ShortDescription: Collective property of phase-coordinated coherence across a CRT-node ensemble within a shared admissibility regime.
Description: Ensemble Synchronization Mode denotes the collective property by which a node ensemble can be characterized in terms of coordinated phase alignment, coherence, and variance reduction across its constituent resonant localized nodes. It does not describe the dynamics of any single node in isolation. Instead, it functions as an ensemble-level order property that expresses the degree to which the multi-node configuration behaves as a coordinated whole under shared admissibility conditions. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a property because it characterizes the ensemble through a derived collective descriptor rather than constituting a new object or a distinct variational pattern. This arising marks the transition from mere multi-node coexistence to an explicitly ordered collective regime and prepares the basis for ensemble-level quanta and effective coarse-grained descriptions at Level 0.

Evidence.Reference: Synchronization and order-parameter formation in ensemble-level admissibility frameworks; structural basis for collective coherence in resonant node ensembles at Level 0.

Name: Ensemble-Scale Quantum Seed
ShortDescription: Object-like ensemble-scale discrete unit emerging from coordinated CRT-node dynamics and preparing the transition to Level 1 field organization.
Description: Ensemble-Scale Quantum Seed denotes the admissible object-like unit formed when coordinated ensemble-level dynamics become sufficiently discrete and stable to function as the immediate precursor of Level-1 field organization. It is not yet a standard-model quantum object and does not claim direct empirical identity with known particles or field quanta. Instead, it marks the final Level-0 transition point at which collective admissibility architecture acquires a stable discrete carrier role capable of enabling the emergence of effective first-level field structure. Within SOMA this arising is classified as an object because it functions as a bounded ensemble-scale unit with persistent identity in the Level-0 to Level-1 transition.

Evidence.Reference: Coarse-graining and collective mode consolidation in ensemble-level admissibility frameworks; structural basis for the emergence of a unified higher-order carrier from synchronized node ensembles at Level 0.

Name: Bridge to Effective First-Level Field
ShortDescription: Effect-level bridge condition in which ensemble-scale discrete organization becomes admissible as the precursor of Level-1 effective field structure.
Description: Bridge to Effective First-Level Field denotes the admissible effect by which ensemble-scale discrete organization becomes sufficient to support the transition into Level-1 effective field structure. It is not itself the empirically established quantum-field domain and should not be read as already identical to Level-1 quantum fields. Instead, it functions as the bridging effect through which Level-0 admissibility architecture produces a coarse-grained precursor compatible with subsequent quantized field organization. Within SOMA this arising is classified as an effect because it expresses the produced transition outcome of ensemble-level closure rather than a stable Level-0 object or a reusable structural pattern. This arising marks the terminal bridge from methodological reconstruction to the first scientifically established field-theoretic level.

Evidence.Reference: Coarse-graining and effective field construction from ensemble-level structural quanta; structural boundary between Level 0 closure and Level 1 field organization.

Name: Proto-field of Admissibility
ShortDescription: Foundational pattern of homogeneous admissibility within which distinguishable differences, ordering, and later Level-0 structures can arise.
Description: Proto-field of Admissibility denotes the foundational homogeneous pattern within which any later Level-0 distinction, compensation, coherence, and localization can become admissible. It is not a physical vacuum, material medium, metric space, or already formed field in the established scientific sense. Instead, it functions as the minimal ontological condition of structural admissibility: a pre-differentiated patterned foundation capable of sustaining later distinguishable variation without yet containing explicit temporal order, spatial separation, localized carriers, or stabilized physical operators. Within SOMA this arising is classified as a pattern rather than an object or property because it does not designate a bounded carrier or a derived attribute, but the repeatable foundational form of homogeneous admissibility itself. This arising should be treated as the explicit predecessor of Field Distinguishability and as the deepest declared Level-0 foundation for subsequent methodological reconstruction.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational ontological reconstruction within SOMA; explicit homogeneous admissibility basis preceding distinguishability and later Level-0 operators.

Name: Minimal Geometric Admissibility Frame
ShortDescription: Minimal geometric property that permits stable ordered differentiation, bounded propagation, and invariant admissibility relations in Level-0 structures.
Description: Minimal Geometric Admissibility Frame denotes the minimal geometric property by which the Proto-field of Admissibility and its first distinguishable variations can sustain ordered differentiation, bounded propagation, and invariant structural comparison. It is not yet a physical spacetime metric, coordinate manifold, or empirical geometry in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the earliest admissible geometric condition under which temporal distinction, spatial distinction, compensation balance, and later wave coherence can become mutually consistent rather than remaining unconstrained variation. This arising is classified as a property because it does not designate a separate carrier or an independent process, but the foundational geometric admissibility condition of an already established patterned domain. It provides the minimal frame in which directional relations, interval-like ordering, and later variational stability become structurally meaningful at Level 0.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational geometric admissibility condition within SOMA; introduced as the minimal frame required for ordered distinction, bounded propagation, and later variational stability.

Name: Discrete Compensation Scale
ShortDescription: Minimal discreteness property by which compensatory balance becomes stably selectable as repeatable quanta of admissible variation.
Description: Discrete Compensation Scale denotes the minimal discreteness property by which compensatory balance becomes stably selectable as repeatable quanta of admissible variation rather than remaining fully continuous and indefinitely divisible. It is not yet a measured physical constant, numerical unit system, or already formalized quantum law in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the earliest admissible scale-property that allows ordered distinction, compensatory cost, and bounded oscillatory support to be stabilized in discrete selectable portions. This arising is classified as a property because it does not designate an independent carrier or a separate process, but the scale condition under which compensation can recur in structurally stable units. In the Level-0 reconstruction this property later supports quantized relations between frequency-like, wave-like, and energy-like expressions, while still remaining an ontological admissibility condition rather than a completed physical formalism.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational discreteness condition within SOMA; introduced as the minimal scale-property by which compensatory balance becomes stably selectable in repeatable units.

Name: Configurational Mass as Integrability Condition
ShortDescription: Integrability property by which bounded coherent configurations acquire stable resistance to dispersion and persistent localized admissibility.
Description: Configurational Mass as Integrability Condition denotes the property by which a bounded coherent configuration acquires stable resistance to dispersion and persistent localized admissibility. It is not yet a material substance, intrinsic particle stuff, or already completed physical mass concept in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the earliest admissible property expressing that closure, compensation, and bounded coherence have become sufficiently integrated to sustain a persistent localized regime rather than a merely propagating or transient oscillatory one. This arising is classified as a property because it does not designate a separate carrier or a distinct process, but the integrability condition of an already formed coherent configuration. In the Level-0 reconstruction it marks the transition from bounded oscillatory support toward stable localized persistence, and later supports relations between closure, configurational density, node formation, and inertia-like admissibility effects.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational integrability condition within SOMA; introduced as the earliest property by which bounded coherent closure can persist as localized admissible support.

Name: Frame-Stable Admissibility Selection
ShortDescription: Property by which admissibility selection remains stable under frame-conditioned variation and supports repeatable bounded configuration filtering.
Description: Frame-Stable Admissibility Selection denotes the property by which admissibility selection remains stable under frame-conditioned variation and can repeatedly filter bounded configurations without collapsing into unconstrained or frame-incompatible fluctuation. It is not a separate variational functional, geometric domain, or already completed physical law in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the property through which the generic admissibility functional F[psi] becomes stably applicable inside the Minimal Geometric Admissibility Frame, so that coherent configurations are not merely evaluated abstractly, but selected in a way that preserves boundedness, repeatability, and compatibility with the foundational frame conditions of Level 0. This arising is classified as a property because it expresses a stabilized condition of selection rather than an independent carrier, object, or process. In the Level-0 reconstruction it strengthens the transition from generic admissibility filtering toward closure-capable and later localization-capable coherent support.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational selection-stability condition within SOMA; introduced as the property by which generic admissibility filtering remains stable inside the minimal geometric admissibility frame.

Name: Configurational Anisotropy
ShortDescription: Property by which a closed configuration exhibits directionally non-uniform organization of bounded support and localized admissibility.
Description: Configurational Anisotropy denotes the property by which a closed configuration exhibits directionally non-uniform organization of bounded support and localized admissibility rather than only scalar concentration. It is not yet a deformation process, external force, or completed physical field asymmetry in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the earliest admissible property expressing that closure, density, and integrability can be organized directionally, so that support is distributed with structurally meaningful orientation instead of remaining isotropically concentrated. This arising is classified as a property because it does not designate an independent object or process, but the directional condition of an already formed coherent and localized configuration. In the Level-0 reconstruction it provides the first explicit bridge from scalar configurational concentration toward directed admissibility structure, and later supports research on closure-preserving deformation, directional admissibility gradients, and localized transport bias.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational directional support condition within SOMA; introduced as the property by which a closed and localized configuration acquires non-isotropic organization of admissible support.

Name: Closure-Preserving Deformation
ShortDescription: Process by which a closed configuration undergoes directional deformation while retaining closure, bounded support, and integrable coherence.
Description: Closure-Preserving Deformation denotes the process by which a closed configuration undergoes directional deformation while retaining closure, bounded support, and integrable coherence. It is not yet arbitrary distortion, destructive perturbation, or externally imposed force response in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the earliest admissible process through which an anisotropically organized configuration can change its internal support distribution without collapsing into loss of resonance, dispersion, or structural disintegration. This arising is classified as a process because it expresses an ongoing transformation of an already closed and localized configuration rather than a static property or independent object. In the Level-0 reconstruction it provides the first explicit dynamic bridge from anisotropic support organization toward directional admissibility gradients, stable reconfiguration, and later localized transport bias.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational deformation process within SOMA; introduced as the earliest process by which anisotropic closed support can change directionally without loss of closure.

Name: Directional Admissibility Gradient
ShortDescription: Property by which admissibility becomes directionally biased, yielding preferred orientation in the stability and transition landscape of a configuration.
Description: Directional Admissibility Gradient denotes the property by which admissibility becomes directionally biased, yielding a preferred orientation in the stability and transition landscape of a configuration. It is not yet a force field, imposed external vector, or completed physical potential gradient in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the earliest admissible property through which anisotropic organization and closure-preserving deformation produce a non-uniform admissibility structure, so that some directions become more favorable than others for persistence, transformation, or propagation. This arising is classified as a property because it expresses a directional condition of the admissibility landscape rather than an independent object or a separate process. In the Level-0 reconstruction it provides the first explicit bridge from directional support reconfiguration toward localized transport bias, field-level anisotropy, and later directional effective organization at higher levels.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational directional-landscape condition within SOMA; introduced as the property by which admissibility acquires a preferred orientation through anisotropic closure-preserving reconfiguration.

Name: Localized Transport Bias
ShortDescription: Effect by which a localized configuration exhibits stable drift or asymmetric transfer feasibility within a directionally biased admissibility landscape.
Description: Localized Transport Bias denotes the effect by which a localized configuration exhibits stable drift or asymmetric transfer feasibility within a directionally biased admissibility landscape. It is not yet propulsion, external thrust, or completed physical transport law in the established scientific sense. Within SOMA it functions as the earliest admissible effect through which directional organization of support, closure-preserving deformation, and a directional admissibility gradient become manifest as a persistent asymmetry in localization, displacement, or transfer feasibility. This arising is classified as an effect because it expresses an observable consequence of prior configurational and admissibility conditions rather than an independent object, carrier, or process. In the Level-0 reconstruction it provides the first explicit terminus of the directional branch and the last local precursor before possible higher-level formulations of effective field anisotropy and directional collective organization.

Evidence.Reference: Foundational directional-transfer effect within SOMA; introduced as the earliest effect by which directionally biased admissibility becomes observable as stable drift or asymmetric transfer feasibility.
